name: Frankie Laine
description: American pop singer, songwriter, and actor (1913–2007)
abstract: Frankie Laine (born Francesco Paolo LoVecchio; March 30, 1913 – February 6, 2007) was an American singer and songwriter whose career spanned nearly 75 years, from his first concerts in 1930 with a marathon dance company to his final performance of "That's My Desire" in 2005. Often billed as "America's Number One Song Stylist", Laine's ot…

about_quotes.speaker: Ronnie Zito
about_quotes.evidence: Frankie Laine is mentioned by the interviewer as a top singer when the interviewee was ten, but the interviewee does not mention any personal interaction, meeting, or specific relationship with Frankie Laine.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ronnie Zito interviewed by Monk Rowe & Rick Montalbano, via Zoom, March 9, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Sherrie Maricle
about_quotes.evidence: he was the drummer with Josephine Baker, Patti Page and Frankie Laine
about_quotes.doc_title: Sherrie Maricle interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 18, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Art Hodes
about_quotes.evidence: So we became a team called Laine and Hodes... Let's join forces.

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: Frankie Laine had contacted me, wrote me a letter, and said he would really like to sing with our big band... Harvey calls at the phone. 'Hello. Frankie Laine. This is Harvey Phillips down in Indiana University, calling about this letter.'
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: he asked me if I could work with him for about a month... who do you think won it, Frankie Laine, won the dance marathon

about_quotes.speaker: Dan D'Imperio
about_quotes.evidence: I got that job playing like Frankie Laine... I wound up playing bass the second time on the Frankie Laine show.
